retrieved.title: Alternative mode of assessment
heading_path: Procedure for claiming an alternative mode of assessment
retrieved.text:
Exams can be modified in two ways to meet your needs based on your impairment:

- O[ption 1](#c639477): Direct communication and an agreement with the lecturer/examiner
- O[ption 2](#c639478): Submitting an application to the directorate of studies (SPL) [JP1] and presenting this application to the lecturer/examiner

In some cases, submitting a formal application ([option 2](#c639478)) can make more sense and/or be necessary. Please discuss your situation with your teachers/examiners and/or the Accessible Studying Team or the responsible directorate of studies. Please read the regulations by the individual directorates of studies (SPL) (links below) and check whether your SPL recommends one of the two options.

Consider which alternative mode of assessment might be most useful and effective for you to outweigh the disadvantages you experience.

- You are welcome to use the counselling offers of the Accessible Studying Team to find suitable (additional) alternative methods for your situation.

1. Discuss the necessary alternative modes of assessment – as soon as possible – with your lecturers/examiners directly and agree on the practical implementation.
2. Together, record what you agreed on in writing (e.g. via e-mail to ensure that all parties involved are well-informed and that the agreement is also transparent for others in the future).
3. If you are asked to provide evidence of your impairment because it is not obvious, please read the [information about the provision of evidence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/) (in German).

retrieved.title: How do I organise my studies?
heading_path: My rights and obligations
retrieved.text:
#### Study law

You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions regarding study law, you can contact the following offices:

- The [directorate of studies](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and organisational tasks during your studies.
- Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of deviating examination methods for students with disabilities. The SPL makes any fundamental decisions in this regard. The [Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei](/en/accessible-studies/)') offers (also anonymous) advice on this.
- The [Office of the Studienpräses](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at) provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the directorate of studies can solve.
- If you have questions about [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) and [tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/), please get in touch with the [Admission Office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/).
- In individual cases, the [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/)) of the Federal Ministry can help find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution concerned.

A degree programme is based on regulations and laws. In the following section you find the most important regulations:

- [Curriculum - the legal basis for degree programmes](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/)
- [Statutes of the University of Vienna - study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

retrieved.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
retrieved.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

retrieved.title: Alternative mode of assessment
retrieved.text:
If your application is granted or if you received a “letter of recommendation” (issued until February 2020), your teachers/examiners are not automatically notified and you are not automatically registered in the relevant university system.

A granted application merely confirms your right to the alternative mode of assessment and provides a solid basis for communication. You have to independently contact your lecturers/examiners in all your courses/exams and inform them that you require an alternative mode of assessment. This is also due to reasons of data protection. Furthermore, some impairments occur in episodes or phases. Therefore, affected students might not have the same needs at all times. An automated system could not take this into account.

Therefore, contact your lecturer or examiner as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

According to section 59, para. 1, item 12 of the [2002 Universities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) (in German), degree programme students and non-degree programme students with an [impairment that directly affects their studies](/en/accessible-studies/) have the right to alternative modes of assessment.

In addition, the Statutes of the University of Vienna specify that there are [equal opportunities for people with a disability and/or a chronic illness](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/equal-opportunities-for-people-with-disabilities-andor-chronic-illnesses/).

You can find the detailed legal texts on the [Accessible University of Vienna portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/weiterfuehrend/rechtliches-und-nachweise/pruefungen/) (in German).

heading_path: Studying safely on-site.
Procedure in case of student accidents
retrieved.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
retrieved.text:
Collapse all

Open all

You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure if you

- have interrupted the programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna and want to [resume your studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)
- [change your teaching subjects](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/#c806587)
- want to be admitted to a [third teaching subject as a complimentary degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/) (only available in German)
- want to voluntarily transfer to the current curriculum or if are automatically transferred to the current curriculum (e.g. if the curriculum expires).

For resuming your studies or changing your teaching subject message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) during the [application period](#c4319). Send a scan of your passport or personal ID and state the teaching subjects you want to study.

Contact the [StudiesServiceCenter Teacher Education](https://ssc-lehrerinnenbildung.univie.ac.at/) if you have questions regarding the transfer to a new curriculum.

reranked.title: How do I organise my studies?
heading_path: My rights and obligations
reranked.text:
#### Study law

You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions regarding study law, you can contact the following offices:

- The [directorate of studies](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and organisational tasks during your studies.
- Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of deviating examination methods for students with disabilities. The SPL makes any fundamental decisions in this regard. The [Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei](/en/accessible-studies/)') offers (also anonymous) advice on this.
- The [Office of the Studienpräses](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at) provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the directorate of studies can solve.
- If you have questions about [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) and [tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/), please get in touch with the [Admission Office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/).
- In individual cases, the [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/)) of the Federal Ministry can help find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution concerned.

A degree programme is based on regulations and laws. In the following section you find the most important regulations:

- [Curriculum - the legal basis for degree programmes](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/)
- [Statutes of the University of Vienna - study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

reranked.title: Accessible entrance exam/aptitude test procedure
heading_path: How do I submit my request for special testing accommodations?
reranked.text:
Submit your application for admission in u:space and pay the administration fee. Select “Submit application.” You will then be registered for the entrance exam procedure/aptitude test.

After you have submitted your application, a window will open in u:space. There you will be informed about the possibility of participating in an evaluation. Further down in the text, you will find a link to the request for accommodations form. Select this link to be redirected to the Servicedesk. Log in to the Servicedesk with your u:account and let us know what accomodations you need. Upload your medical specialist assessments. We also accept assessments of psychotherapists and clinical psychologists.

Do you have questions on requesting special testing accommodations? Please reach out to us via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/64/create/1800).

You will receive written notice via e-mail within a few days. Each type of disability is unique. Adaptations to testing will be made on a case-by-case basis. Hence, we will coordinate any special accommodations and their technical implementation with you. We will then inform you about the next steps in the entrance exam/aptitude test procedure.

reranked.title: Alternative mode of assessment
reranked.text:
If your application is granted or if you received a “letter of recommendation” (issued until February 2020), your teachers/examiners are not automatically notified and you are not automatically registered in the relevant university system.

A granted application merely confirms your right to the alternative mode of assessment and provides a solid basis for communication. You have to independently contact your lecturers/examiners in all your courses/exams and inform them that you require an alternative mode of assessment. This is also due to reasons of data protection. Furthermore, some impairments occur in episodes or phases. Therefore, affected students might not have the same needs at all times. An automated system could not take this into account.

Therefore, contact your lecturer or examiner as soon as possible to discuss your needs.

According to section 59, para. 1, item 12 of the [2002 Universities Act](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128) (in German), degree programme students and non-degree programme students with an [impairment that directly affects their studies](/en/accessible-studies/) have the right to alternative modes of assessment.

In addition, the Statutes of the University of Vienna specify that there are [equal opportunities for people with a disability and/or a chronic illness](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/more-parts-of-the-statutes/equal-opportunities-for-people-with-disabilities-andor-chronic-illnesses/).

You can find the detailed legal texts on the [Accessible University of Vienna portal](https://barrierefrei.univie.ac.at/weiterfuehrend/rechtliches-und-nachweise/pruefungen/) (in German).

Procedure in case of student accidents
reranked.text: What should I do if a fellow student or I have an accident while studying? Accidents have to be reported. Please note that accident reports concerning students in connection with their studies must be submitted electronically. We ask you to please NO longer use any previous PDF forms or copy templates that you may have saved. The most recent version of the form “Accident report for persons in educational institutions” can be used by students who have had an accident at the university or on the way to or from the university. It is completed via the AUVA website ([auva.at/unfallmeldung](http://auva.at/unfallmeldung)). 1. Students fill out the form and select the option “Forward at the educational institution”. 2. Students enter the name and email address of the director of studies. If the accident occurred in a service facility, e.g. in the university library, USI, etc., then the email goes to the DLE management there. In cases of doubt, the report can also be sent to the management of the DLE Study Services and Teaching ([slw.leitung@univie.ac.at](mailto:slw.leitung@univie.ac.at)). 3. The head of the department or the head of the division will be notified and will then forward this form electronically to the AUVA. 4. If you have any questions about the accident report, the AUVA service team will be happy to help at any time. Contact the AUVA at: E-Mail: [wla@auva.at](mailto:wla@auva.at), Tel.: +43 5 9393

Please note: The admission to the Teacher Education Programme with the new teaching subject is only in effect starting from the semester start (1 October/1 March). ##### Changing to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education If you want to change to the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education, you have to register for the bachelor programme Sport and Human Movement Science via u:space and pay 50 Euro for the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) within the [application period](/en/admission/application-and-admission-periods/#c1068202). For technical reasons, it is not possible to register for the teaching subject Sports and Physical Education via u:space. If you have successfully participated in the sporting aptitude admission test, send us a message in which you indicate the desired change via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/936). The Admission Office will effectuate the change, which you will see after a few days in u:space at Studies > Study overview. You

heading_path: Exemptions for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education
Active Teachers
reranked.text:
You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure for the bachelor's programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna if you fulfil the following:

- You work as teacher at a primary or secondary school with publics rights within the EU/EEA or Switzerland.

**Follow these steps:**

1. Create your u:account in [u:space](http://uspace.univie.ac.at/en). Make sure you remember your login data. You will need the u:account during your studies. Log into u:space and complete the registration.
2. Send the following proofs via the [Servicedesk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/62/create/935)
   1. all pages of your secondary-school leaving certificate/degree certificate merged into one PDF file
      including translation if the document was not issued in German or English
   2. a confirmation about your work issued by the headmaster
3. State which subjects you want to study in your message.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: W
reranked.text: / - Waiting list and allocation of remaining places (courses) After the end of a registration deadline, you can check whether or not you gained a place on a lecture/an exam in u:space under Studies > Registration Overview. Furthermore, you receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. If you did not gain a place, you may be listed on the waiting list or be offered an alternative parallel course. If students deregister from a course/exam and a waiting list exists, you can move up the waiting list. If you are on the waiting list, please attend the first unit of the course as there may be free places. In case of long waiting lists, the directorate of studies (SPL) will probably offer additional parallel courses, for which you may register. In this case, you will be able to change the number of points you allocated before a second allocation may start. If you gained an alternative place (remaining place) in a parallel course for which remaining places are available, you have to confirm or reject your place within the deadline defined by the directorate of studies (SPL). If you reject the place, you will be automatically deregistered from the course. The allocated points can not be transferred to the next semester. If you neither confirm nor reject the place, you remain on the waiting list. Related Links: - [Registration

reranked.text:

Open all

You are exempt from the aptitude test procedure if you

- have interrupted the programme in Teacher Education at the University of Vienna and want to [resume your studies](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/same-degree-programme/)
- [change your teaching subjects](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/en/admission/change-of-degree-programme/change-of-bachelordiploma-programme/#c806587)
- want to be admitted to a [third teaching subject as a complimentary degree programme](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/zulassung/lehramtsstudien/erweiterungsstudium/) (only available in German)
- want to voluntarily transfer to the current curriculum or if are automatically transferred to the current curriculum (e.g. if the curriculum expires).

For resuming your studies or changing your teaching subject message us via the [contact form](/en/contact) during the [application period](#c4319). Send a scan of your passport or personal ID and state the teaching subjects you want to study.

Contact the [StudiesServiceCenter Teacher Education](https://ssc-lehrerinnenbildung.univie.ac.at/) if you have questions regarding the transfer to a new curriculum.

selected.title: Alternative mode of assessment
heading_path: Procedure for claiming an alternative mode of assessment
selected.text:
Exams can be modified in two ways to meet your needs based on your impairment:

- O[ption 1](#c639477): Direct communication and an agreement with the lecturer/examiner
- O[ption 2](#c639478): Submitting an application to the directorate of studies (SPL) [JP1] and presenting this application to the lecturer/examiner

In some cases, submitting a formal application ([option 2](#c639478)) can make more sense and/or be necessary. Please discuss your situation with your teachers/examiners and/or the Accessible Studying Team or the responsible directorate of studies. Please read the regulations by the individual directorates of studies (SPL) (links below) and check whether your SPL recommends one of the two options.

Consider which alternative mode of assessment might be most useful and effective for you to outweigh the disadvantages you experience.

- You are welcome to use the counselling offers of the Accessible Studying Team to find suitable (additional) alternative methods for your situation.

1. Discuss the necessary alternative modes of assessment – as soon as possible – with your lecturers/examiners directly and agree on the practical implementation.
2. Together, record what you agreed on in writing (e.g. via e-mail to ensure that all parties involved are well-informed and that the agreement is also transparent for others in the future).
3. If you are asked to provide evidence of your impairment because it is not obvious, please read the [information about the provision of evidence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/) (in German).

selected.title: How do I organise my studies?
heading_path: My rights and obligations
selected.text:
#### Study law

You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions regarding study law, you can contact the following offices:

- The [directorate of studies](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and organisational tasks during your studies.
- Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of deviating examination methods for students with disabilities. The SPL makes any fundamental decisions in this regard. The [Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei](/en/accessible-studies/)') offers (also anonymous) advice on this.
- The [Office of the Studienpräses](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at) provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the directorate of studies can solve.
- If you have questions about [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) and [tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/), please get in touch with the [Admission Office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/).
- In individual cases, the [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/)) of the Federal Ministry can help find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution concerned.

A degree programme is based on regulations and laws. In the following section you find the most important regulations:

- [Curriculum - the legal basis for degree programmes](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/)
- [Statutes of the University of Vienna - study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

selected.title: Changes to exams and teaching
selected.text:
Many students and prospective students with impairments are entitled to request exam adaptations or alternative modes of assessment. Read the information provided below to find out whether you are eligible, how the application and/or implementation works and what types of evidence you have to provide.

- [Exam adaptations for the entrance exam procedure (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/aufnahmeverfahren/aufnahme-eignungsverfahren-mit-beeintraechtigung/)

- [Alternative modes of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)
- [Modified curriculum (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/modifiziertes-curriculum/)
- [Discontinuing exams or courses for an important reason (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/)

By changing the mode of assessment, it is possible to outweigh many disadvantages resulting from an impairment. © Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

For many adaptations and support offers, you have to provide evidence to the University of Vienna of how the impairment manifests itself and how it affects your studies. Please note the general information about types of evidence you have to present to the University of Vienna.

[Link to further information about evidence of impairment](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/)

selected.title: ABC of terminology
heading_path: N
selected.text: required). A resumption of the study programme is not possible. Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in mode of assessment that was not announced). You cannot appeal against the content-related assessment as this is at the examiner's discretion. In the event of serious irregularities at the time of the exam, you can apply for a deletion of your grade/attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within four weeks after grading. The plausibility of the serious irregularities has to be demonstrated. A proof is not necessary. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Related Links: - [Office of the Studienpräses (in German)](https://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at/) - [Study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/) Related entries: - [Board examination](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31160&cHash=97b4b803d01ac1fde81b560e8d2ab5fa) - Non-degree programme A non-degree programme means that you are admitted to “attending individual courses”. In this case, you do not study any particular degree programme. Especially persons who have to complete supplementary exams as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) are admitted to a non-degree programme. As part of the university entrance qualification examination(Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without a secondary-school leaving certificate), non-degree programme students can also complete courses. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [University Preparation Programme of the Vienna

You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

- O[ption 2](#c639478): Submitting an application to the directorate of studies (SPL) [JP1] and presenting this application to the lecturer/examiner

In some cases, submitting a formal application ([option 2](#c639478)) can make more sense and/or be necessary. Please discuss your situation with your teachers/examiners and/or the Accessible Studying Team or the responsible directorate of studies. Please read the regulations by the individual directorates of studies (SPL) (links below) and check whether your SPL recommends one of the two options.

Consider which alternative mode of assessment might be most useful and effective for you to outweigh the disadvantages you experience.

- You are welcome to use the counselling offers of the Accessible Studying Team to find suitable (additional) alternative methods for your situation.

1. Discuss the necessary alternative modes of assessment – as soon as possible – with your lecturers/examiners directly and agree on the practical implementation.
2. Together, record what you agreed on in writing (e.g. via e-mail to ensure that all parties involved are well-informed and that the agreement is also transparent for others in the future).
3. If you are asked to provide evidence of your impairment because it is not obvious, please read the [information about the provision of evidence](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/) (in German).

[4] How do I organise my studies? — My rights and obligations
#### Study law

You have rights and obligations that govern your studies from admission until graduation. If you have any questions regarding study law, you can contact the following offices:

- The [directorate of studies](http://www.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/governance-structure/organisation-of-studies-courses/) responsible for your degree programme should be your first point of contact if you have any questions regarding legal issues and organisational tasks during your studies.
- Lecturers and examiners are the primary contact persons for the implementation of deviating examination methods for students with disabilities. The SPL makes any fundamental decisions in this regard. The [Accessible Studying Team ('Team Barrierefrei](/en/accessible-studies/)') offers (also anonymous) advice on this.
- The [Office of the Studienpräses](http://studienpraeses.univie.ac.at) provides support if you encounter problems during your studies that neither your lecturer nor the directorate of studies can solve.
- If you have questions about [admission](/en/admission-procedure/) and [tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/), please get in touch with the [Admission Office](https://slw.univie.ac.at/en/about-us/admission-office/).
- In individual cases, the [Ombuds office for students ('Ombudsstelle für Studierende](https://hochschulombudsstelle.at/)) of the Federal Ministry can help find a solution together with the bodies and members of the educational institution concerned.

A degree programme is based on regulations and laws. In the following section you find the most important regulations:

- [Curriculum - the legal basis for degree programmes](https://senat.univie.ac.at/curricularkommission/curricula/)
- [Statutes of the University of Vienna - study law](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [University law (2002 Universities Act, Part II: Study Law)](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

[5] Changes to exams and teaching
Many students and prospective students with impairments are entitled to request exam adaptations or alternative modes of assessment. Read the information provided below to find out whether you are eligible, how the application and/or implementation works and what types of evidence you have to provide.

- [Exam adaptations for the entrance exam procedure (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/aufnahmeverfahren/aufnahme-eignungsverfahren-mit-beeintraechtigung/)

- [Alternative modes of assessment](/en/accessible-studies/changes-to-exams-and-teaching/alternative-mode-of-assessment/)
- [Modified curriculum (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/modifiziertes-curriculum/)
- [Discontinuing exams or courses for an important reason (in German)](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/abbruch-von-pruefungen-oder-lehrveranstaltungen-aus-wichtigem-grund/)

By changing the mode of assessment, it is possible to outweigh many disadvantages resulting from an impairment. © Universität Wien / Barbara Mair

For many adaptations and support offers, you have to provide evidence to the University of Vienna of how the impairment manifests itself and how it affects your studies. Please note the general information about types of evidence you have to present to the University of Vienna.

[Link to further information about evidence of impairment](https://studieren.univie.ac.at/barrierefrei-studieren/anpassungen-bei-pruefungenlven/nachweis-der-beeintraechtigung/)

Appeal against a negative assessment You can appeal against a negative grade if there were serious irregularities in the conduct of the exam (e.g. reduced time, noise, disturbance, change in mode of assessment that was not announced). You cannot appeal against the content-related assessment as this is at the examiner's discretion. In the event of serious irregularities at the time of the exam, you can apply for a deletion of your grade/attempt in the Office of the Studienpräses within four weeks after grading. The plausibility of the serious irregularities has to be demonstrated. A proof is not necessary. You will receive an official notice, notifying you whether or not your application was successful. Especially persons who have to complete supplementary exams as part of the University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (Vorstudienlehrgang der Wiener Universitäten, VWU) are admitted to a non-degree programme. As part of the university entrance qualification examination(Studienberechtigungsprüfung – studying without a secondary-school leaving certificate), non-degree programme students can also complete courses.
